successCriteria.description: src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exists and exports AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput with fields id, entityType, entityId, and action.

src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exists and exports AuditRepository with create(input) and findByEntity(entityType, entityId) methods returning AuditRecord-based types imported from audit.model.ts.
A PostgreSqlAuditRepository contract is exported and conforms to the AuditRepository interface without introducing circular dependencies.

The canonical PostgreSQL schema definition for audit_records is included exactly as specified: id UUID PRIMARY KEY, entity_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, entity_id UUID NOT NULL, action VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL.
tests/unit/modules/audit/audit.repository.spec.ts exists, uses Vitest, and verifies contract exports and TypeScript type compatibility for AuditRecord, CreateAuditRecordInput, and AuditRepository.

constraints: Limit implementation strictly to audit persistence contracts and related type definitions/tests.
Follow the repository pattern: all future database access must go through repository interfaces.
Repository method signatures must use AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput types from src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts.
Do not implement audit business workflows, leave services, API endpoints, or database persistence logic in this phase.
Use TypeScript, Node.js 20, npm, and Vitest conventions used by the project.
Document or preserve the canonical audit_records schema exactly as provided.
Transaction semantics are limited to separate audit repository operations; do not introduce multi-entity atomic transactions in this phase.
No unhandled promise rejections; any async stubs or contracts must be compatible with the project's error-handling standard.
outOfScope: Leave approval or rejection workflow logic
Fastify API routes, controllers, RBAC enforcement implementation, or request validation
LeaveBalance domain models, repositories, or balance deduction logic
Notification domain models, repositories, or workflow processing
Concrete PostgreSQL persistence implementation beyond repository contracts/stubs
Any files outside the audit module contract definitions, schema declaration, and unit tests

ambiguities.description: The architecture notes specify PostgreSqlAuditRepository as an abstract class, while the detailed architecture specifies it as a concrete class with not-implemented methods.
options: Export PostgreSqlAuditRepository as an abstract class implementing AuditRepository
Export PostgreSqlAuditRepository as a concrete class implementing AuditRepository with methods that throw 'Not implemented in Phase 2'
